fields.description: Layer that the rule belongs to identified by the name or UID.

fields.label: limit
fields.description: No more than that many results will be returned. This parameter is relevant only for getting few objects. Valid values are between 1 and 500.

fields.description: Object name. Should be unique in the domain.

fields.label: offset
fields.description: Skip that many results before beginning to return them. This parameter is relevant only for getting few objects.

fields.label: package
fields.description: Name of the package.

fields.label: show as ranges
fields.description:
When true, the source, destination and services & applications parameters are displayed as ranges of IP addresses and port numbers rather than network objects.
Objects that are not represented using IP addresses or port numbers are presented as objects.
In addition, the response of each rule does not contain the parameters, source, source-negate, destination, destination-negate, service and service-negate, but instead it contains the parameters, source-ranges, destination-ranges and service-ranges.

Note, Requesting to show rules as ranges is limited up to 20 rules per request, otherwise an error is returned. If you wish to request more rules, use the offset and limit parameters to limit your request. Available from R80.20 management version.

fields.label: show hits
fields.description: N/A

fields.label: show membership
fields.description: Indicates whether to calculate and show "groups" field for every object in reply. Available from R80.10 management version.

fields.label: use object dictionary
fields.description: N/A

fields.description: Set to be true in order to install the Desktop Security policy. By default, the value is true if desktop security policy is enabled on the input policy package, otherwise false. Available from R80.20.M1 management version.

fields.label: install on all cluster members or fail
fields.description: Relevant for the gateway clusters. If true, the policy is installed on all the cluster members. If the installation on a cluster member fails, don't install on that cluster. Available from R80.10 management version.

fields.label: policy package
fields.description: The name of the Policy Package to be installed.

fields.label: prepare only
fields.description: If true, prepares the policy for the installation, but doesn't install it on an installation target. Available from R80.10 management version.

fields.label: qos
fields.description: Set to be true in order to install the QoS policy. By default, the value is true if Quality-of-Service policy is enabled on the input policy package, otherwise false. Available from R80.20.M1 management version.

fields.label: revision
fields.description: The UID of the revision of the policy to install. Available from R80.10 management version.

fields.label: targets
fields.description: On what targets to execute this command. Targets may be identified by their name, or object unique identifier.

fields.label: threat prevention
fields.description: Set to be true in order to install the Threat Prevention policy. By default, the value is true if Threat Prevention policy is enabled on the input policy package, otherwise false.

fields.description: Name or UID of the required default profile for all global domains.

fields.description: Name or UID of the required default profile for all local domains.

fields.label: details level
fields.description: The level of detail for some of the fields in the response can vary from showing only the UID value of the object to a fully detailed representation of the object.

fields.label: domains to process
fields.description: Indicates which domains to process the commands on. It cannot be used with the details-level full, must be run from the System Domain only and with ignore-warnings true. Valid values are, CURRENT_DOMAIN, ALL_DOMAINS_ON_THIS_SERVER.

fields.label: enable default profile for global domains
fields.description: Enable the option to specify a default profile for all global domains.

fields.label: enable default profile for local domains
fields.description: Enable the option to specify a default profile for all local domains.

fields.label: global vpn management
fields.description:
Lets the administrator select Enable global use for a Security Gateway shown in the MDS Gateways & Servers view.
Only a 'Manager' permission-level profile can edit this permission.

fields.label: ignore errors
fields.description: Apply changes ignoring errors. You won't be able to publish such a changes. If ignore-warnings flag was omitted - warnings will also be ignored.

fields.label: ignore warnings
fields.description: Apply changes ignoring warnings.

fields.label: manage admins
fields.description:
Create and manage Multi-Domain Security Management administrators with the same or lower permission level. For example, a Domain manager cannot create Superusers or global managers.
Only a 'Manager' permission-level profile can edit this permission.

fields.label: manage global assignments
fields.description: Controls the ability to create, edit and delete global assignment and not the ability to reassign, which is set according to the specific Domain's permission profile.

fields.label: manage sessions
fields.description:
Connect/disconnect Domain sessions, publish changes, and delete other administrator sessions.
Only a 'Manager' permission-level profile can edit this permission.

fields.label: management api login
fields.description:
Permission to log in to the Security Management Server and run API commands using these tools, mgmt_cli (Linux and Windows binaries), Gaia CLI (clish) and Web Services (REST). Useful if you want to prevent administrators from running automatic scripts on the Management.
Note, This permission is not required to run commands from within the API terminal in SmartConsole.

fields.label: mds provisioning
fields.description:
Create and manage Multi-Domain Servers and Multi-Domain Log Servers.
Only a "Super User" permission-level profile can select this option.

fields.label: name
fields.description: Object name.

fields.label: permission level
fields.description:
The level of the Multi Domain Permissions Profile.
The level cannot be changed after creation.

fields.label: tags
fields.description: Collection of tag identifiers.

fields.label: version
fields.description: Version of checkpoint. If not given one, the latest version taken.

fields.label: view global objects in domain
fields.description: Lets an administrator with no global objects permissions view the global objects in the domain. This option is required for valid domain management.

fields.description: Wait for the task to end. Such as publish task.

fields.description: How many minutes to wait until throwing a timeout error.

fields.label: domains to process
fields.description: Indicates which domains to process the commands on. It cannot be used with the details-level full, must be run from the System Domain only and with ignore-warnings true. Valid values are, CURRENT_DOMAIN, ALL_DOMAINS_ON_THIS_SERVER. Available from R81 management version.

fields.label: filter
fields.description: Search expression to filter objects by. The provided text should be exactly the same as it would be given in Smart Console. The logical operators in the expression ('AND', 'OR') should be provided in capital letters. By default, the search involves both a textual search and a IP search. To use IP search only, set the "ip-only" parameter to true. Available from R80.10 management version.

fields.label: ip only
fields.description:
If using "filter", use this field to search objects by their IP address only, without involving the textual search.

IP search use cases
    
  • Full IPv4 address matches for,
             - Hosts, Check Point Hosts and Gateways with exact IPv4 match or with interfaces which subnet contains the search address
             - IPv4 Networks and IPv4 Address Ranges that contain the search address

    •     
  • Partial IPv4 address matches for,
             - Hosts, Networks, Check Point Hosts and Gateways with IPv4 address that starts from the search address
             - Hosts, Check Point Hosts and Gateways with interfaces which subnet address starts from the search address
             - IPv4 Address Ranges with first address or last address that starts from the search address
             - IPv4 Networks and IPv4 Address Ranges that contain the network derived from the search address supplemented with missing octets (all zeroes)
             - Hosts, Check Point Hosts and Gateways with interfaces which subnet contains the network derived from the search address supplemented with missing octets (all zeroes)

    •     
  • IPv6 address,
             - Not supported


* Check Point Host is a server of type Network Policy Management, Logging & Status, SmartEvent, etc.
* When one IP address is checked to start from another (partial) IP address - only full octets are considered
* Check Examples part for IP search examples. Available from R80.10 management version.

fields.description: Publish the current session if changes have been performed after task completes.

fields.description: Apply changes ignoring warnings.

fields.label: ip protocol
fields.description: IP protocol number.

fields.label: keep connections open after policy installation
fields.description: Keep connections open after policy has been installed even if they are not allowed under the new policy. This overrides the settings in the Connection Persistence page. If you change this property, the change will not affect open connections, but only future connections.

fields.label: match
fields.description: Contains an INSPECT expression that defines the matching criteria. The connection is examined against the expression during the first packet. Example, tcp, dport = 21, direction = 0 matches incoming FTP control connections. Available from R80.10 management version.

fields.label: match for any
fields.description: Indicates whether this service is used when 'Any' is set as the rule's service and there are several service objects with the same source port and protocol.

fields.label: override default settings
fields.description: Indicates whether this service is a Data Domain service which has been overridden. Available from R80.10 management version.

fields.label: session timeout
fields.description: Time (in seconds) before the session times out.

fields.description: State of the access rule (present or absent).

fields.description: Enables state-synchronized High Availability or Load Sharing on a ClusterXL or OPSEC-certified cluster.

fields.description: Collection of tag identifiers.

fields.label: use default session timeout

fields.label: keep connections open after policy installation
fields.description: Keep connections open after policy has been installed even if they are not allowed under the new policy. This overrides the settings in the Connection Persistence page. If you change this property, the change will not affect open connections, but only future connections.

fields.label: match by protocol signature
fields.description: A value of true enables matching by the selected protocol's signature - the signature identifies the protocol as genuine. Select this option to limit the port to the specified protocol. If the selected protocol does not support matching by signature, this field cannot be set to true. Available from R80.10 management version.

fields.label: match for any
fields.description: Indicates whether this service is used when 'Any' is set as the rule's service and there are several service objects with the same source port and protocol.

fields.label: name
fields.description: Object name.

fields.label: override default settings
fields.description: Indicates whether this service is a Data Domain service which has been overridden. Available from R80.10 management version.

fields.label: protocol
fields.description: Select the protocol type associated with the service, and by implication, the management server (if any) that enforces Content Security and Authentication for the service. Selecting a Protocol Type invokes the specific protocol handlers for each protocol type, thus enabling higher level of security by parsing the protocol, and higher level of connectivity by tracking dynamic actions (such as opening of ports). Available from R80.10 management version.

fields.label: session timeout
fields.description: Time (in seconds) before the session times out.

fields.label: source port
fields.description: Port number for the client side service. If specified, only those Source port Numbers will be Accepted, Dropped, or Rejected during packet inspection. Otherwise, the source port is not inspected.

fields.label: state

modules.label: cp_mgmt_set_app_control_advanced_settings
modules.tooltip: Edit Application Control & URL Filtering Blades' Settings.

fields.label: auto publish session
fields.description: Publish the current session if changes have been performed after task completes.

fields.label: block request when web service is unavailable
fields.description: Block requests when the web service is unavailable. When selected, requests are blocked when there is no connectivity to the Check Point Online Web Service. When cleared, requests are allowed when there is no connectivity. This property is not available in the Global domain of an MDS machine.

fields.label: categorize social network widgets
fields.description: When selected, the Security Gateway connects to the Check Point Online Web Service to identify social networking widgets that it does not recognize. When cleared or there is no connectivity between the Security Gateway and the Check Point Online Web, the unknown widget is treated as Web Browsing traffic. This property is not available in the Global domain of an MDS machine.

fields.label: domain level permission
fields.description: Allows the editing of applications, categories, and services. This property is used only in the Global Domain of an MDS machine.

fields.label: enable web browsing
fields.description: If you do not enable URL Filtering on the Security Gateway, you can use a generic Web browser application called Web Browsing in the rule. This application includes all HTTP traffic that is not a defined application Application and URL Filtering assigns Web Browsing as the default application for all HTTP traffic that does not match an application in the Application and URL Filtering Database. This property is not available in the Global domain of an MDS machine.

fields.label: httpi non standard ports
fields.description: Enable HTTP inspection on non standard ports for application and URL filtering. This property is not available in the Global domain of an MDS machine.

fields.label: internal error fail mode
fields.description: In case of internal system error, allow or block all connections. This property is not available in the Global domain of an MDS machine.

fields.label: match application on any port
fields.description: Match Web application on 'Any' port when used in Block rule - By default this is set to true. and so applications are matched on all services when used in a Block rule.

fields.label: version
fields.description: Version of checkpoint. If not given one, the latest version taken.

fields.label: wait for task
fields.description: Wait for the task to end. Such as publish task.

fields.label: wait for task timeout
fields.description: How many minutes to wait until throwing a timeout error.

fields.label: web browsing services
fields.description: Web browsing services are the services that match a Web-based custom Application/Site.

fields.label: website categorization mode
fields.description: Hold - Requests are blocked until categorization is complete. Background - Requests are allowed until categorization is complete. Custom - configure different settings depending on the service. Lets you set different modes for URL Filtering and Social Networking Widgets. This property is not available in the Global domain of an MDS machine.

fields.description: Controls when the partial scan starts after publishing a session. The partial scan checks only the relevant firewall best practices. If the value is < 0, the partial scan is disabled. If the value is 0, the partial scan starts immediately after publishing. If the value is > 0, the partial scan is delayed by the specified number of seconds after publishing. The default value is '0'.

fields.label: blocked certificate tracking
fields.description:
Controls whether to log and send a notification for dropped traffic.
  • None - Does not record the event.
  • Log - Records the event details in SmartView.
  • Alert - Logs the event and executes a command.
  • Mail - Sends an email to the administrator.
  • SNMP Trap - Sends an SNMP alert to the SNMP GU.
  • User Defined Alert - Sends customized alerts.
.

fields.label: bypass on client failure
fields.description:
Whether all requests should be bypassed or blocked-in case of client errors (Client closes the connection due to authentication issues during handshake)
  • true - Fail-open (bypass all requests).
  • false - Fail-close (block all requests.

The default value is true.

fields.label: bypass on failure
fields.description:
Whether all requests should be bypassed or blocked-in case of server errors (for example validation error during GW-Server authentication)
  • true - Fail-open (bypass all requests).
  • false - Fail-close (block all requests.

The default value is true.

fields.label: bypass update services
fields.description:
Configure the value "true" to bypass traffic to well-known software update services.
The default value is true.

fields.label: certificate pinned apps action
fields.description:
Configure the value "bypass" to bypass traffic from certificate-pinned applications approved by Check Point.
HTTPS Inspection cannot inspect connections initiated by certificate-pinned applications.
Configure the value "detect" to send logs for traffic from certificate-pinned applications approved by Check Point.
The default value is bypass.

fields.label: details level
fields.description: The level of detail for some of the fields in the response can vary from showing only the UID value of the object to a fully detailed representation of the object.

fields.label: domains to process
fields.description: Indicates which domains to process the commands on. It cannot be used with the details-level full, must be run from the System Domain only and with ignore-warnings true. Valid values are, CURRENT_DOMAIN, ALL_DOMAINS_ON_THIS_SERVER.

fields.label: ignore errors
fields.description: Apply changes ignoring errors. You won't be able to publish such a changes. If ignore-warnings flag was omitted - warnings will also be ignored.

fields.label: ignore warnings
fields.description: Apply changes ignoring warnings.

fields.label: log sessions
fields.description:
The value "true" configures the Security Gateway to send HTTPS Inspection session logs.
The default value is true.

fields.label: retrieve intermediate ca certificates
fields.description:
Configure the value "true" to use the "Certificate Authority Information Access" extension to retrieve certificates that are missing from the certificate chain.
The default value is true.

fields.label: site categorization allow mode
fields.description:
Whether all requests should be allowed or blocked until categorization is complete.
  • Background - to allow requests until categorization is complete.
  • Hold- to block requests until categorization is complete.

The default value is hold.

fields.label: filter
fields.description: Search expression to filter objects by. The provided text should be exactly the same as it would be given in SmartConsole Object Explorer. The logical operators in the expression ('AND', 'OR') should be provided in capital letters. The search involves both a IP search and a textual search in name, comment, tags etc. Available from R81 JHF management version.

modules.label: cp_mgmt_test_sic_status
modules.tooltip: Test SIC Status reflects the state of the gateway after it has received the certificate issued by the ICA. If the SIC status is Unknown then there is no connection between the gateway and the Security Management Server. If the SIC status is No Communication, an error message will appear. It may contain specific instructions on how to fix the situation.

fields.label: auto publish session
fields.description: Publish the current session if changes have been performed after task completes.

fields.label: color
fields.description: Color of the object. Should be one of existing colors.

fields.label: comments
fields.description: Comments string.

fields.label: details level
fields.description: The level of detail for some of the fields in the response can vary from showing only the UID value of the object to a fully detailed representation of the object.

fields.label: domains assignment
fields.description: Domains to be added to this profile. Use domain name only. See example below, "add-trusted-client (with domain)".

fields.label: ignore errors
fields.description: Apply changes ignoring errors. You won't be able to publish such a changes. If ignore-warnings flag was omitted - warnings will also be ignored.

fields.label: ignore warnings
fields.description: Apply changes ignoring warnings.

fields.label: ip address
fields.description: IPv4 or IPv6 address. If both addresses are required use ipv4-address and ipv6-address fields explicitly.

fields.label: ip address first
fields.description: First IP address in the range. If both IPv4 and IPv6 address ranges are required, use the ipv4-address-first and the ipv6-address-first fields instead.

fields.label: ip address last
fields.description: Last IP address in the range. If both IPv4 and IPv6 address ranges are required, use the ipv4-address-first and the ipv6-address-first fields instead.

fields.label: ipv4 address
fields.description: IPv4 address.

fields.label: ipv4 address first
fields.description: First IPv4 address in the range.

fields.label: ipv4 address last
fields.description: Last IPv4 address in the range.

fields.label: ipv6 address
fields.description: IPv6 address.

fields.label: ipv6 address first
fields.description: First IPv6 address in the range.

fields.label: ipv6 address last
fields.description: Last IPv6 address in the range.

fields.label: mask length
fields.description: IPv4 or IPv6 mask length. If both masks are required use mask-length4 and mask-length6 fields explicitly.

fields.label: mask length4
fields.description: IPv4 mask length.

fields.label: mask length6
fields.description: IPv6 mask length.

fields.label: multi domain server trusted client
fields.description: Let this trusted client connect to all Multi-Domain Servers in the deployment.

fields.description: Object name.

fields.label: state
fields.description: State of the access rule (present or absent).

fields.label: tags
fields.description: Collection of tag identifiers.

fields.label: type
fields.description: Trusted client type.

fields.label: version
fields.description: Version of checkpoint. If not given one, the latest version taken.

fields.label: wait for task
fields.description: Wait for the task to end. Such as publish task.

fields.description: How many minutes to wait until throwing a timeout error.

fields.label: wild card
fields.description: IP wild card (e.g. 192.0.2.*).

modules.label: cp_mgmt_vsx_provisioning_tool
modules.tooltip: Run the VSX provisioning tool with the specified parameters.

fields.label: auto publish session
fields.description: Publish the current session if changes have been performed after task completes.

fields.label: operation
fields.description:
The name of the provisioning operation to run. Each operation has its own specific parameters.
The available operations are,
  • add-vsx-gateway - Adds a new VSX gateway
  • add-vsx-cluster - Adds a new VSX cluster*
  • add-vsx-cluster-member - Adds a new VSX cluster member*
  • add-vd - Adds a new Virtual Device (VS/VSB/VSW/VR) to a VSX gateway or VSX cluster
  • add-vd-interface - Adds a new virtual interface to a Virtual Device
  • add-physical-interface - Adds a physical interface to a VSX gateway or VSX cluster
  • add-route - Adds a route to a Virtual Device
  • attach-bridge - Attaches a bridge interface to a Virtual System
  • remove-vsx - Removes a VSX gateway or VSX cluster
  • remove-vd - Removes a Virtual Device
  • remove-vd-interface - Removes an interface from a Virtual Device
  • remove-physical-interface - Removes a physical interface from a VSX gateway or VSX cluster
  • remove-route - Removes a route from a Virtual Device
  • set-vd - Modifies a Virtual Device
  • set-vd-interface - Modifies an interface on a Virtual Device
  • set-physical-interface - Modifies a physical interface on a VSX cluster or VSX gateway

* When adding a VSX Cluster, you must also add at least 2 cluster members
* Adding cluster members is only allowed when adding a new VSX cluster
* To add members to an existing cluster, use vsx-run-operation.

fields.label: version
fields.description: Version of checkpoint. If not given one, the latest version taken.

fields.label: wait for task
fields.description: Wait for the task to end. Such as publish task.

fields.label: wait for task timeout
fields.description: How many minutes to wait until throwing a timeout error.

modules.label: cp_mgmt_vsx_run_operation
modules.tooltip: Run the VSX operation by its name and parameters.

fields.label: auto publish session
fields.description: Publish the current session if changes have been performed after task completes.

fields.label: operation
fields.description:
The name of the operation to run. Each operation has its specific parameters.
The available operations are,
  • upgrade - Upgrades the VSX Gateway or VSX Cluster object to a higher version
  • downgrade - Downgrades the VSX Gateway or VSX Cluster object to a lower version
  • add-member - Adds a new VSX Cluster member object
  • remove-member - Removes a VSX Cluster member object
  • reconf-gw - Reconfigures a VSX Gateway after a clean install
  • reconf-member - Reconfigures a VSX Cluster member after a clean install
.
